programs.name: Delaware Medicaid for Elderly and Disabled
programs.what_it_does: Provides medical assistance to eligible seniors and individuals with disabilities. Covers services like doctor visits, hospital care, prescription drugs, and long-term care.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Delaware residents aged 65+ or aged 18-64 with disabilities meeting financial and medical criteria.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by eligibility; covers eligible medical costs up to 100% for qualified individuals.

programs.name: Diamond State Health Plan Plus (DSHP Plus) Waivers
programs.what_it_does: Offers home and community-based services to help seniors and disabled individuals avoid institutional care. Includes personal care, homemaker services, and home modifications.
programs.who_its_for: Medicaid-eligible Delaware residents aged 65+ or 18+ with disabilities at risk of nursing home placement.
programs.benefit_value: Annual services up to $60,000 per participant depending on waiver program.

programs.name: Delaware PACE Program
programs.what_it_does: Delivers comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings. Includes adult day health care, transportation, and home care to prevent nursing home admission.
programs.who_its_for: Delaware residents aged 55+ needing nursing home level care but able to live safely in the community.
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive capitated Medicare/Medicaid coverage; value equivalent to institutional care costs.

programs.name: Delaware Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: Pays Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copayments for eligible low-income seniors. QMB covers Part A/B premiums and costs; SLMB/QI cover Part B premiums.
programs.who_its_for: Delaware Medicare beneficiaries with income below 100-135% FPL and limited assets.
programs.benefit_value: QMB: Full Medicare cost coverage (~$7,500/year); SLMB: Part B premium ($185/month); QI: Partial premium assistance.

programs.name: Delaware Food First Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly benefits via EBT card for purchasing groceries. Helps low-income seniors maintain nutritional health.
programs.who_its_for: Delaware residents aged 60+ with income ≤165% FPL; no asset test for most seniors.

programs.name: Delaware Home Energy Assistance Program (HEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Assists with heating and electric bills during winter months. Provides one-time payments to energy vendors on behalf of eligible households.
programs.who_its_for: Delaware households aged 60+ with income ≤200% FPL including seniors/disabled.
programs.benefit_value: $200-$1,000 one-time benefit based on household size/income.

programs.name: Delaware Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Improves energy efficiency of homes through insulation, sealing, and heating system repairs. Reduces energy costs and health risks from poor housing conditions.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Delaware households aged 60+ (priority) with income ≤200% FPL.
programs.benefit_value: Free services valued at $5,000-$10,000 per home.

programs.name: Delaware Senior Medicare Patrol (SMP)
programs.what_it_does: Educates Medicare beneficiaries on detecting fraud, waste, and abuse. Provides free counseling to resolve Medicare billing issues.
programs.who_its_for: All Delaware Medicare beneficiaries, especially seniors 65+.
programs.benefit_value: Free counseling; recovers thousands in improper Medicare charges annually.

programs.name: Delaware Home Delivered Meals
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors through Area Agencies on Aging. Promotes independence and reduces malnutrition risk.
programs.who_its_for: Delaware residents aged 60+ who are homebound and unable to prepare meals.

programs.name: CARE Delaware Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides respite care, counseling, and support groups for family caregivers. Offers breaks from caregiving responsibilities and training.
programs.who_its_for: Delaware caregivers aged 60+ caring for impaired adults, or seniors 55+ raising relatives' children.
programs.benefit_value: Respite grants up to $1,600/year; counseling and support groups.

programs.name: Delaware Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time paid work experience (20 hours/week) at community sites. Includes job training and placement assistance for unsubsidized employment.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage pay (~$15/hour DE); training valued at $7,500/year.

programs.name: Delaware Legal Services for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal assistance for issues like housing, benefits, powers of attorney, and consumer protection. Operated through DSAAPD and legal aid partners.
programs.who_its_for: Delaware residents aged 60+ with low income facing legal issues.
programs.benefit_value: Free legal services; pro bono representation value $200+/hour.

programs.name: Delaware Long Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes, assisted living, and similar facilities. Investigates complaints and resolves issues regarding care quality and rights.
programs.who_its_for: Delaware long-term care residents and their families; serves all ages.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy; resolves 80%+ of complaints without litigation.

programs.name: Delaware Prescription Assistance Program (DPAP)
programs.what_it_does: DPAP provides a prescription drug benefit of up to $3,000 per year for eligible seniors and disabled individuals. Qualified participants pay a co-payment of $5 or 25% of the cost, whichever is greater, and must have Medicare Part D.
programs.who_its_for: Delaware seniors or disabled individuals with low income who have Medicare Part D.
programs.whats_unique: State-funded pharmaceutical assistance program offering up to $3,000 annual prescription coverage specifically for low-income Delaware Medicare enrollees, beyond federal Medicare Part D.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $3,000 per year in prescription drug coverage

programs.name: Nursing Home Transition Program
programs.what_it_does: This state-funded program identifies and assists nursing home residents wishing to return to community living through individualized case management. It supports transitions especially for Medicaid-eligible individuals.
programs.who_its_for: Nursing home residents in Delaware, particularly Medicaid-eligible elderly or disabled adults, seeking community-based settings.
programs.whats_unique: Delaware state-funded initiative led by DSAAPD focused solely on facilitating nursing home discharges to community settings with personalized support.
programs.benefit_value: Individualized case management for community transition

programs.name: Wilmington Senior Tax Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: The program provides grants to help senior homeowners in Wilmington avoid foreclosure due to unpaid property taxes, sewer, or water bills. It offers financial relief up to a maximum grant amount.
programs.who_its_for: Wilmington homeowners aged 62 or older facing imminent foreclosure from delinquent taxes or utility bills.
programs.whats_unique: City-specific (Wilmington, DE) state-assisted tax relief program targeting senior foreclosure prevention, not available statewide or in other states.

programs.name: SCAT – Senior Citizens Affordable Taxi
programs.what_it_does: SCAT offers discounted taxi fares to make transportation more accessible. Seniors and disabled individuals receive a 50% reduction on fares.
programs.who_its_for: Delaware senior citizens and persons with disabilities.
programs.whats_unique: Delaware-specific 50% taxi fare discount program for seniors, providing state-supported affordable local transportation.

programs.name: Delaware Foster Grandparent Program
programs.what_it_does: This program places senior volunteers in daycares, Head Starts, schools, and detention centers to mentor and tutor children with special needs. It engages seniors in meaningful community service.
programs.who_its_for: Delaware citizens aged 60 years or older.
programs.whats_unique: State-operated volunteer program uniquely positioning Delaware seniors as mentors for exceptional needs children in educational settings.
programs.benefit_value: Volunteer placement opportunities enhancing purpose and community involvement

programs.name: Over-60 Tuition Benefit
programs.what_it_does: Delaware residents aged 60 and older can pursue undergraduate or graduate degrees tuition-free at the University of Delaware upon admission. It supports lifelong learning on a part-time or full-time basis.
programs.who_its_for: Delaware residents aged 60 or older admitted to UD degree programs.
programs.whats_unique: State resident-specific tuition waiver at University of Delaware for seniors, excluding non-degree or non-credit courses.
programs.benefit_value: Tuition-free undergraduate or graduate degree programs
